task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
trace: The function first checks if the input list is empty and returns an empty string if true. It then sorts the list to ensure that consecutive numbers are adjacent. Using a loop, it iterates through the sorted list, tracking the start and end of consecutive sequences. When it encounters a number not consecutive to the current end, it appends the appropriate range (or single number) to the result list. Finally, it handles the last range before joining all parts with commas to form the final string.
